topic: Skill tool pattern across AI agent harnesses — what it IS, how it varies, and whether @agent-sh/harness-* should ship one

sources.title: Agent Skills Specification
keyInsights: Open spec (originally Anthropic, now community): SKILL.md + optional scripts/, references/, assets/
Required frontmatter: name (1-64 chars, [a-z0-9-], no leading/trailing hyphen, no double hyphens, must match parent directory name), description (1-1024 chars)
Optional frontmatter: license, compatibility (1-500 chars), metadata (key/value map), allowed-tools (experimental)
Progressive disclosure: tier 1 metadata (~100 tokens always-in-context), tier 2 SKILL.md body (<5000 tokens on activation), tier 3 resources (unbounded, on reference)
Recommend keeping SKILL.md under 500 lines; split detailed reference material to separate files
skills-ref reference library at github.com/agentskills/agentskills/tree/main/skills-ref validates SKILL.md

sources.title: Agent Skills — What are skills?
keyInsights: Progressive disclosure is three phases: Discovery (startup, name+desc only), Activation (body loaded when task matches), Execution (bundled files loaded as needed)
Self-documenting, extensible, portable — the three design principles
Skills are just files — easy to edit, version, and share

sources.title: Adding skills support to your agent
keyInsights: Five-phase lifecycle: discover → parse → disclose → activate → manage over time
Four activation patterns: (A) dedicated tool, (B) file-read via Read tool, (C) system prompt injection, (D) slash command — harness picks one or more
Universal convention: .agents/skills/ for cross-client interop, .<your-client>/skills/ for client-specific, pragmatic fallback .claude/skills/
Name collision rule: project-level > user-level, log warning on collision
Trust consideration: gate project-level skill loading on 'user trusts this directory' (same as workspace trust)
Lenient validation: warn on name/directory mismatch but load, skip if YAML unparseable or description missing
Catalog placement: either system prompt section OR tool description (both valid)
If dedicated tool: constrain name parameter to enum of valid skill names to prevent hallucination
Protect skill content from compaction (wrap in identifying tags, flag as durable)

sources.title: Claude Code — Extend Claude with skills
keyInsights: Custom commands (.claude/commands/*.md) merged into skills; both create /name and work the same

Claude Code extends the open Agent Skills standard with: invocation control (disable-model-invocation, user-invocable), subagent execution (context: fork, agent:), dynamic context injection (!`<cmd>`), allowed-tools pre-approval, paths glob gating, $ARGUMENTS/$N substitutions, ${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID}/${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}
Skill location precedence: enterprise > personal (~/.claude/skills/) > project (.claude/skills/); plugin skills namespaced as plugin-name:skill-name
Live change detection via filesystem watch; nested .claude/skills/ in monorepo packages auto-discovered
Skill content lifecycle: rendered SKILL.md enters conversation as one message and stays until session end; NOT re-read on later turns
Auto-compaction: re-attaches most-recent invocation of each invoked skill, keeping first 5k tokens per skill, 25k total budget
Description cap: 1,536 chars combined description+when_to_use in skill listing; default budget ~8k chars scaling to 1% of context window; override via SLASH_COMMAND_TOOL_CHAR_BUDGET env var
Subagent preload: 'skills' field on subagent injects full skill content at startup (inverse of context: fork)

sources.title: Claude Code Tools reference (Skill tool listed)
keyInsights: Skill is listed as a built-in tool with Permission: Yes
Description: 'Executes a skill within the main conversation'
Skills run through the existing Skill tool — not a new tool entry per skill
Permission patterns: Skill (deny all), Skill(name) exact match, Skill(name *) prefix match

sources.title: Anthropic Platform — Agent Skills overview
keyInsights: Three surfaces: Claude API (with code execution + skills beta headers), Claude Code (filesystem-based, custom only), Claude.ai (zip upload via Settings)
API requires three beta headers: code-execution-2025-08-25, skills-2025-10-02, files-api-2025-04-14
name validation: max 64 chars, lowercase/numbers/hyphens only, NO XML tags, NO reserved words (anthropic, claude)
description validation: non-empty, max 1024 chars, NO XML tags
Skills are NOT eligible for ZDR (Zero Data Retention)
Custom skills do NOT sync across surfaces — must upload separately to each
Claude.ai: individual user only, no centralized admin; API: workspace-wide; Claude Code: personal or project or plugin-bundled
API runtime: NO network, NO runtime package install (pre-configured deps only); Claude Code: full network, discourage global package install; Claude.ai: variable network per admin settings
Strong security warning: malicious skills can direct Claude to invoke tools or execute code, treat like installing software

sources.title: Anthropic Platform — Skill authoring best practices
keyInsights: Context window is a public good; even SKILL.md body tokens compete with conversation
'Default assumption: Claude is already very smart. Only add context Claude doesn't already have.'
Set appropriate degrees of freedom: high (text), medium (pseudocode), low (specific scripts)
Test with every model you plan to use — Haiku needs more guidance than Opus
Naming convention: prefer gerund form (processing-pdfs, analyzing-spreadsheets); avoid helper/utils/tools
Write descriptions in THIRD PERSON ('Processes Excel files' not 'I can help you')
Each skill has exactly ONE description field — make it the discovery signal
Progressive disclosure patterns: (1) high-level guide with references, (2) domain-specific organization (BigQuery by domain), (3) conditional details
Keep references ONE level deep from SKILL.md — Claude partially reads deeply-nested files
Reference files >100 lines: include a table of contents at top
Workflows for complex tasks (copy-this-checklist pattern), feedback loops (run validator → fix → repeat)
Avoid time-sensitive info, use consistent terminology, avoid too many options (provide a default + escape hatch)
Scripts should 'solve, don't punt' — handle errors explicitly, document magic numbers
Utility scripts save tokens (code never in context, only output)
Plan-validate-execute pattern for batch operations (intermediate JSON, script-verified before execute)
Evaluation-driven development: measure unskilled baseline, write just enough to pass evals, iterate

sources.title: Anthropic engineering: Equipping agents for the real world with Agent Skills
keyInsights: Published Oct 16 2025; core argument: composable specialization vs fragmented custom agents
Agents need 'procedural knowledge and organizational context' for real-world work
Three-tier information architecture prevents context bloat
'Amount of context that can be bundled into a skill is effectively unbounded' via progressive disclosure
MCP is complementary: skills operate at higher abstraction, teaching workflows that may use MCP tools
Skills replace monolithic instruction sets with modular, discoverable knowledge structures

sources.title: Anthropic: Introducing Skills (news announcement)
keyInsights: Announcement date: October 16, 2025. Update Dec 18, 2025: organization-wide management, directory, open standard published

Four key characteristics: composable (multiple skills work together), portable (same format across Claude apps/Code/API), efficient (minimal info loaded when relevant), powerful (can include executable code)
'Skills are folders that include instructions, scripts, and resources that Claude can load when needed'
skill-creator skill guides users through folder structure and SKILL.md creation

sources.title: Simon Willison: Claude Skills are awesome, maybe a bigger deal than MCP
keyInsights: Third-party independent analysis; key headline: 'maybe a bigger deal than MCP'
MCP token cost argument: GitHub's MCP consumes 'tens of thousands of tokens of context', skills cost 'few dozen extra tokens' per skill
Framing: Claude Code is 'a tool for general computer automation', not just coding
Universality: 'You can grab a skills folder right now, point Codex CLI or Gemini CLI at it and say read pdf/SKILL.md.'
Skills leverage the coding environment itself — LLMs already know --help, file navigation, shell
Concern raised: sandboxing to prevent prompt injection attacks

sources.title: OpenCode tools overview (skill tool in 13-tool surface)
keyInsights: 13 tools: read, write, edit, apply_patch, grep, glob, lsp (experimental), bash, webfetch, websearch, skill, todowrite, question
skill tool: 'Load a skill (a SKILL.md file) and return its content in the conversation'
permission.skill config: allow/deny/ask
skill is positioned between pure code tools and user interaction capabilities

sources.title: OpenCode skills documentation
keyInsights: Discovery order: project first (.opencode/skills/, .claude/skills/, .agents/skills/), then global (~/.config/opencode/skills/, ~/.claude/skills/, ~/.agents/skills/)
Walks up directory tree to git worktree root
Strict name validation: regex ^[a-z0-9]+(-[a-z0-9]+)*$, must match directory name
Frontmatter: name (1-64 chars), description (1-1024 chars), optional license/compatibility/metadata
Permission rules (allow/deny/ask) configured in opencode.json with pattern matching
Can disable entirely via tools.skill: false
Agent frontmatter can override permissions per-agent

sources.title: OpenCode skill.ts source
keyInsights: Input schema: { name: string }
Description loaded from external skill.txt file
Lookup via Skill.Service dependency; errors list all available skills on not-found
Permission check via 'skill' permission type with skill name as pattern BEFORE loading
Uses Ripgrep to sample up to 10 files in the skill directory (excluding SKILL.md)
Returns: formatted skill content block + base directory URL + sampled files + metadata
Emphasizes sandboxed access control and limited file discovery

sources.title: Continue.dev readSkill source
keyInsights: Input schema: { skillName: string } (required)
displayTitle: 'Read Skill'; readonly: true; isInstant: true
Loads markdown skills via loadMarkdownSkills(params.ide)
Description dynamically lists available skills with name + description
Returns markdown content with {{{ skillName }}} templating for user-facing messages

sources.title: Gemini CLI activate-skill source
keyInsights: Input type: ActivateSkillToolParams { name: string }
Schema dynamically generated via getActivateSkillDefinition(skillNames) — enum of valid skill names
Display title: 'Activate Skill'
Returns ToolResult with llmContent: <activated_skill> XML containing body + folder resources
Error messages include list of available skills
Key pattern: enum-constrained schema prevents hallucinated skill names

sources.title: Codex CLI skills documentation
keyInsights: NO dedicated Skill tool — skills are a packaging format, not a discrete invocation mechanism
Two activation approaches: (1) explicit via `$skill-name` mention or `/skills` listing, (2) implicit via description-match auto-selection
Directory structure: SKILL.md required + optional scripts/, references/, assets/, agents/openai.yaml
Auto-detection of skill changes (live reload); new installs may require restart
Progressive disclosure: Codex starts with metadata only, loads full SKILL.md only when activated
$ prefix mentions skills directly in prompts; `$skill-creator` and `$skill-installer` are bundled

sources.title: Codex core-skills Rust crate
keyInsights: Public types: SkillMetadata, SkillPolicy, SkillError, SkillLoadOutcome, SkillsLoadInput, AvailableSkills, SkillMetadataBudget, SkillDependencyInfo
Functions: detect_implicit_skill_invocation_for_command(), build_skill_name_counts(), build_available_skills()
Modules: loader, manager, model, render, injection, config_rules, remote
Confirms Codex's architecture: system-prompt injection of catalog + pattern-based command detection + NO tool call
Has 'remote' module suggesting API-fetched skills (not just local filesystem)

sources.title: Claude Code subagents docs (skills integration)
keyInsights: Subagents: specialized AI assistants with own context window, custom system prompt, specific tool access, independent permissions
Built-in subagents: Explore (Haiku, read-only), Plan (read-only, for plan mode), general-purpose (all tools)
Subagent frontmatter includes: name, description, tools, disallowedTools, model, permissionMode, maxTurns, skills, mcpServers, hooks, memory, background, effort, isolation, color, initialPrompt
`skills` field preloads skill bodies INTO the subagent's context at startup — full content, not just catalog
Cannot preload skills with disable-model-invocation: true
Subagents do NOT inherit skills from parent; must list explicitly
Subagent with `skills: []` field vs skill with `context: fork` — inverse directions of same composition
Permission modes: default, acceptEdits, auto, dontAsk, bypassPermissions, plan
Bypass-permissions still protects .git/.claude/.vscode/.idea/.husky (except .claude/commands, .claude/agents, .claude/skills)

sources.title: Claude Code MCP documentation
keyInsights: MCP is for custom tool connection; skills are for reusable prompt-based workflows
ToolSearch + defer_loading scale MCP tool counts by NOT putting all tool schemas in context at startup
MCP servers remain the right answer for typed function-call surfaces
Explicit guidance: 'Write a skill, which runs through the existing Skill tool rather than adding a new tool entry'

sources.title: Claude Agent SDK overview
keyInsights: Agent SDK renamed from Claude Code SDK; wraps the same built-in tools
Default built-in tools: Read, Write, Edit, Bash, Monitor, Glob, Grep, WebSearch, WebFetch, AskUserQuestion
Skills loaded from .claude/skills/*/SKILL.md by default (configurable via setting_sources)
Skills, slash commands, memory (CLAUDE.md), plugins are all filesystem-based SDK features
allowedTools option lets you opt in/out at runtime
Same three-tier disclosure as Claude Code

sources.title: Amp manual (Agent Skills section)
keyInsights: NO dedicated Skill tool — skills integrate transparently via catalog visibility to model
Name + description always visible to model; body loaded only on invocation (Pattern B)
Discovery precedence: ~/.config/agents/skills/, ~/.config/amp/skills/, .agents/skills/, .claude/skills/, ~/.claude/skills/, plugins, toolbox, built-in
Frontmatter: required name + description; optional tools, severity-default
mcp.json inside skill directory keeps MCP tools hidden until skill is loaded
Installation via command palette or CLI; from GitHub, git URLs, or local paths

sources.title: Cursor skills documentation
keyInsights: Adopts agentskills.io standard with disable-model-invocation extension
Discovery: .agents/skills/, .cursor/skills/, ~/.agents/skills/, ~/.cursor/skills/, fallback to legacy Claude/Codex dirs
Two activation pathways: automatic detection via description-matching, manual via / in Agent chat
Built-in migration tool: /migrate-to-skills converts dynamic rules and slash commands to skills format, preserving explicit invocation
disable-model-invocation: true makes a skill behave like a traditional slash command

sources.title: Junie (JetBrains) agent skills
keyInsights: NO dedicated Skill tool — skills are auto-invoked based on task description matching
Discovery: .junie/skills/ project-level, ~/.junie/skills/ user-level (or %USERPROFILE%\.junie\skills\ on Windows)
Frontmatter: name + description required
Skills activate only when needed (unlike always-applied guidelines)
Project-level overrides user-level
Skills portable: both Junie CLI and JetBrains IDE plugin read them

sources.title: OpenHands skills documentation
keyInsights: Skills are 'specialized prompts' with domain knowledge; not a tool call
Three activation modes: always-on (AGENTS.md at start), keyword-triggered (frontmatter match), agent-invoked
Discovery: .agents/skills/ (preferred), deprecated .openhands/skills/ and .openhands/microagents/, ~/.agents/skills/
Frontmatter only required for keyword-triggered skills
Community registry on GitHub for sharing skills — organizational/global
Decentralized, file-based system with multiple activation modes vs Claude Code's explicit tool invocation

sources.title: Roo Code skills documentation
keyInsights: NO dedicated Skill tool — skills are instruction packages, not executable tools
Directory locations: ~/.roo/skills/, .roo/skills/, mode-specific skills-{mode}/ (e.g., skills-code/)
Project overrides global; .roo/ overrides .agents/ at same level
Required frontmatter: name (1-64 chars), description (1-1024 chars)
Three-tier progressive disclosure: frontmatter discovery → full content via read_file → bundled resources on demand
'Skills remain dormant until activated — they don't bloat your base prompt'
Mode-specific skills: Roo-specific extension (architect, code, debug modes)

sources.title: anthropics/skills reference repository
keyInsights: Top-level: ./skills/ (organized by category), ./spec/, ./template/, .claude-plugin/
Skill categories: Creative & Design, Development & Technical, Enterprise & Communication, Document (PDF/DOCX/PPTX/XLSX)
License split: most skills Apache 2.0; document creation/editing skills source-available (not open source)
122k stars by Q2 2026 — the ecosystem's central reference
Disclaimer: demonstration/educational only; test thoroughly before relying on for critical tasks
Basic structure matches spec: YAML frontmatter + markdown body

sources.title: agentskills.io — adopter carousel (ecosystem breadth)
keyInsights: ~35+ adopters by Q2 2026: Junie, Gemini CLI, Autohand, OpenCode, OpenHands, Mux, Cursor, Amp, Letta, Firebender, Goose, GitHub Copilot, VS Code, Claude Code, Claude, OpenAI Codex, Piebald, Factory, pi, Databricks Genie, Agentman, TRAE, Spring AI, Roo Code, Mistral AI Vibe, Command Code, Ona, VT Code, Qodo, Laravel Boost, Emdash, Snowflake Cortex Code, Kiro, Workshop, Google AI Edge Gallery, nanobot, fast-agent
Spec originally developed by Anthropic, released as open standard, community-maintained via github.com/agentskills/agentskills
Discussion on GitHub Discussions + Discord

sources.title: Goose (Block) skills guide
keyInsights: Block's open-source extensible agent adopts the spec
Positioned under 'context engineering' — framing as a context-management tool
Pattern B-ish: catalog-based, not a tool call

sources.title: Letta skills documentation
keyInsights: Letta Code implements the open Agent Skills standard
Multi-scope: project (.agents/skills/), agent-scoped (~/.letta/agents/{id}/skills/), global (~/.letta/skills/)
Stateful-agent platform — different context model from coding agents but same skill primitive

sources.title: Kiro skills documentation
keyInsights: Spec-driven development platform adopts skills
Framing: skills bring structure to AI coding

sources.title: Firebender skills (Android-native coding agent)
keyInsights: Android-native coding agent; skills for multi-agent orchestration
Proves spec's vertical applicability (mobile development)

sources.title: Factory CLI skills
keyInsights: AI-native software dev platform with Droids — delegates refactors, incidents, migrations
CI/CD-integrated skills

sources.title: GitHub Copilot agent skills
keyInsights: GitHub's adoption proves broad cross-vendor acceptance
Same SKILL.md format; Copilot uses the standard unmodified

sources.title: VS Code Copilot Agent Skills
keyInsights: Microsoft/VS Code adoption of the spec
Same progressive disclosure model
IDE-integrated skills

sources.title: Spring AI generic agent skills
keyInsights: Spring AI (Java ecosystem) adopts the spec in Jan 2026
Proves the spec's language-neutrality — not tied to TS/Python

sources.title: Laravel Boost agent skills
keyInsights: Framework-bundled skills model: Laravel ships skills with the framework for AI-assisted dev
Proves the 'library bundles skills' distribution pattern

sources.title: Databricks Genie Code skills
keyInsights: Data-platform vertical adoption (Databricks)
Genie Code uses skills for extensibility

sources.title: Emdash skills documentation
keyInsights: Desktop app for parallel coding agents with git worktree isolation
Skills composable across parallel agents

sources.title: fast-agent skills documentation
keyInsights: ACP-integrated framework for LLMs; skills development explicitly supported
Good for 'Coding, Evals, ACPX and Skills development'

sources.title: Qodo agent skills case study
keyInsights: PR-automation use case for skills
Auto-fix-in-PR workflow via skills
Concrete end-user scenario showing skill composition
